methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc

data.narrative:
SemiQon is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Himadri Majumdar and Janne Lehtinen that has raised €19.5M to date. The quantum computing sector encompasses a modest but meaningful comp universe of 11 total peers: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Application of standard triangulation methods is constrained by data sparsity. The peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient granular clustering: across the 7 same-sector peers identified, no combination of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters yielded the minimum 3 datapoints required for robust median estimation. A full-sector median would conflate heterogeneous development stages and business models, introducing unacceptable noise.

The analysis therefore cannot support a single point valuation or narrow range at this time. A credible valuation framework would require either (1) expansion of comparable data among Series A quantum firms, (2) application of bottom-up methods anchored to SemiQon's specific technical roadmap and capital efficiency, or (3) staged monitoring of recent financings among the identified private peers to establish timing-relevant benchmarks. The key uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient same-stage comparables to calibrate investor return expectations for early-stage quantum hardware development.
